key_risks: Sticky core PCE at 3.4% YoY could push hawkish FOMC minutes (July 8) and July decision, pressuring rate-sensitive financials and regional banks
Bank Q2 earnings (JPM, WFC) could disappoint on credit or NII, reversing the recent financials/regional-bank rally that relies partly on momentum
Weak June jobs report (July 2) could trigger risk-off, hurting cyclical industrials and small-cap-heavy regional banks
Tech/AI rebound could cause equal-weight and defensive sectors to lag the cap-weighted S&P 500 benchmark

portfolio.rationale: Strong 30d relative outperformance (+8.3% vs SPY), Q2 bank earnings (JPM, WFC July 14) as catalyst, low volatility, high up-day share; sticky rates at 3.50-3.75% support net interest margins.
ISM manufacturing still expansionary at 53.3, new orders robust at 56.0; strong benchmark-relative performance (+8.05% 30d) with moderate volatility supports continuation.
Defensive sector leading recently (+9.8% vs SPY 30d), low beta 0.37 provides downside protection against tech-led index weakness while offering positive momentum.
Best up-day share (80.95%), strong 30d relative return (+13.6%), benefits from bank earnings season and stable rate environment; limited fundamental support beyond macro, higher volatility noted.
Broad diversified US large-cap exposure that reduces mega-cap tech concentration risk, positive 30d relative return, lower beta 0.74; hedges against AI/tech reversal dragging cap-weighted index.

portfolio_rationale: Tilt toward cyclical value and defensive sectors showing strong benchmark-relative momentum backed by resilient macro data (ISM 53.3, stable Fed) and near-term bank earnings catalysts, while underweighting rich-valuation mega-cap tech that dragged indexes lower into entry.
rationale_summary: Overweight financials, industrials, healthcare, and equal-weight to capture broadening leadership and bank earnings while diversifying away from tech concentration risk.
